title: Secrets Of A Successful Marriage

jokes.text: Don't try to eat these so-called 'chips.'
jokes.setup: Homer is at a poker game with actual poker chips
jokes.punchline: He's been trying to eat the poker chips thinking they're food
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - perfectly in character and gets the absurdity across efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... poker chips.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Homer, those are the poker chips!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: You're eating all our chips, man.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's what they're there for, right? To eat?

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: No, Homer. We play with them.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, I already ate them all.

jokes.text: D'oh! D'oh! D'oh! Uh, I mean, whoo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Homer gets good cards but doesn't realize it
jokes.punchline: He tries to cover his disappointed reaction when he realizes the cards are actually good
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's inability to understand poker strategy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh man, what a terrible hand. I got nothing. I'm gonna lose so much money.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... these are... these are good cards! Royal flush!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I mean... uh... these stink. Worst hand ever. I'm so bummed.

jokes.text: You have a straight flush, Homer. G'oh! You do this every time, ya-- Oh, you--
jokes.setup: Homer wins despite being clueless
jokes.punchline: Barney's frustrated reaction to Homer's oblivious luck
jokes.explanation: Standard frustrated reaction comedy

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: How does that idiot keep winning? He doesn't even know what he's doing!

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Meanwhile, I'm here actually trying, and I can't catch a break. This is so unfair!

jokes.text: Chokin' on my own rage, here.
jokes.setup: Moe is frustrated with Homer's poker incompetence
jokes.punchline: He describes his anger in an overly dramatic yet understated way
jokes.explanation: Great line that's very quotable and perfectly captures Moe's character voice

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer, you're so bad at poker, I'm not even mad. I'm just... disappointed. Actually, no, I am mad. I'm furious. My blood is boiling. I'm experiencing a level of rage that hasn't been seen since the invention of anger itself. But you know what? It's fine. Everything's fine.

jokes.text: Hey, Homer, you still here? Boy, you are slow.
jokes.setup: Homer is still processing the insult
jokes.punchline: Lenny doubles down, confirming Homer's slowness in real time
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reinforces the joke and Homer's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Yeah, Homer, you're not the brightest bulb in the box.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey! I resent that!

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: You're resenting it? It took you three minutes to understand what I said.

jokes.text: 'a little slow'! [Laughing Loudly]
jokes.setup: Homer tells Marge about being insulted
jokes.punchline: He finds the insult hilarious instead of offensive
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's complete lack of self-awareness in a believable way

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, you won't believe what Lenny said to me at work today. He called me an idiot!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, that's terrible! What did you do?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, at first I was mad, but then I realized... he's right! And it's actually pretty funny!

jokes.text: Marge and Lisa's non-verbal response to Homer's question
jokes.setup: Homer asks if they think he's slow
jokes.punchline: Their uncomfortable silence and stammering confirms it
jokes.explanation: Effective use of silence and awkwardness to deliver the uncomfortable truth

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You guys think I'm slow?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, I... that is to say...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you're not... I mean, everyone has their own...

jokes.text: How come you're not laughing? Do you think I'm slow? - Buh. - Snuh.
jokes.setup: Homer expects family to laugh at the 'ridiculous' insult
jokes.punchline: Family's uncomfortable non-verbal reactions
jokes.explanation: Perfect uncomfortable family reaction beat

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why you little—

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What? I was just gonna say something ridiculous!

jokes.text: It's those TV networks, Marge. They won't let me--
jokes.setup: Homer claims he wants to read and go to museums
jokes.punchline: He blames TV networks for stopping him, breaking into meta-commentary about television
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke that breaks the fourth wall while staying in character

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I want to read more books and go to museums.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But the TV networks keep me from doing it with their compelling programming!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: If only they'd show less interesting stuff, I'd have time for self-improvement.

jokes.text: But they won't! They won't let me live! [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Homer's rant about TV networks continues
jokes.punchline: He becomes genuinely emotional about this absurd conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that takes the meta-joke to an emotional extreme

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The networks don't want you to know this, but it's all connected. They're watching us through the TV, man. Watching and waiting. And they're laughing at us!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: My whole life... everything I've done... it's all been for their entertainment. *voice breaking* And I never even got paid!

jokes.text: Take another bath in malt liquor?
jokes.setup: Marge suggests ways to feel better about himself
jokes.punchline: Homer's first suggestion is an absurd and unhealthy activity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's approach to self-improvement with absurd specificity

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: You could take up a hobby, Homer. Something to make you feel better about yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I could start eating cubed butter.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: No, Homer, that's not healthy.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, stop crushing my dreams!

jokes.text: Every time I learn something new, it pushes some old stuff out of my brain.
jokes.setup: Homer questions the value of education
jokes.punchline: He thinks his brain has limited storage space like a computer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and quotable line that became a cultural reference. Perfect character logic with universal relatability

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: My brain has a limited storage capacity. I've had to forget the smallest states just to make room for new useless information.

jokes.text: Remember when I took that home wine-making course and I forgot how to drive?
jokes.setup: Homer gives an example of learning pushing out old knowledge
jokes.punchline: The example actually proves the opposite point - he was just drunk
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Homer's flawed logic while maintaining character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, brain like a sponge. You know, you learn something new, an old thing just gets pushed right out of the back of your head.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Remember that time I took a home winemaking course and I forgot how to drive?

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: That wasn't from the course, Homer. You were drunk.

jokes.text: That's because you were drunk! - And how.
jokes.setup: Marge points out the obvious flaw in Homer's logic
jokes.punchline: Homer cheerfully agrees, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Homer's obliviousness to logical contradictions

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that doesn't make any sense!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You're right, Marge. I don't make sense.

jokes.text: At this point I'd like to remind you there are no refunds.
jokes.setup: Students react with disgust to the instructor's advice about tight clothes
jokes.punchline: Instructor calmly states the no-refund policy amid the chaos
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery in the face of student revolt

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Now, I know what you're thinking - 'Won't tight clothes restrict my movement?' Well, yes. But that's a small price to pay for looking fantastic.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Ugh, that's disgusting!

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Also, I should mention - all sales are final. No refunds.

jokes.text: You see, that ping sound means the spit was on target.
jokes.setup: An instructor is teaching spitting technique
jokes.punchline: He treats spitting with scientific precision and methodology
jokes.explanation: Absurd class offering that satirizes adult education programs

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Now, the key to proper spitting is all about trajectory and velocity. You need to calculate the angle of projection, account for wind resistance, and maintain consistent saliva viscosity.

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Let's break down the mechanics: a 45-degree angle gives you maximum distance, but for accuracy, you'll want to adjust based on target proximity and ambient humidity levels.

dialogue.speaker: Instructor
dialogue.line: Class, I've developed a formula. Distance equals initial velocity squared, times the sine of twice the launch angle, divided by gravitational acceleration.

jokes.text: I can tell the difference between butter and I Can't Believe It's Not Butter.
jokes.setup: Homer is asked about his area of expertise
jokes.punchline: He claims to have a skill that's actually impossible/meaningless
jokes.explanation: Good product reference that shows Homer's delusional confidence

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I'm an expert at doing nothing while appearing to work.

jokes.text: No, you can't, Mr. Simpson. No one can.
jokes.setup: Homer claims butter expertise
jokes.punchline: Administrator matter-of-factly destroys Homer's delusion
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that deflates Homer's pretension efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm an expert on butter. I've been studying it for years.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: Homer, you work at a nuclear power plant. You've never studied butter in your life.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I've eaten a lot of it.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: That doesn't make you an expert. That makes you fat.

jokes.text: That depends. Is there another way to get this job?
jokes.setup: Administrator asks if Homer is married when discussing marriage counseling job
jokes.punchline: Homer is willing to lie about being married if it helps
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's opportunistic dishonesty in character-appropriate way

dialogue.speaker: Administrator
dialogue.line: Are you married?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, but I'm willing to lie about it.

jokes.text: Anything to get me out of that house... away from all that nagging and noise... uh, of a family of love. 'Sha-la-la-la'
jokes.setup: Homer eagerly accepts the job
jokes.punchline: He starts to reveal his real motivations then awkwardly covers
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Homer's poor filter and quick cover-up

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'd be perfect for this job! I'm a hard worker, I'm dedicated, I'm... I'm just excited to be around all those donuts all day. I mean, uh, to serve the public! Yeah, that's what I meant.

jokes.text: Ah, incorrect, Marge-- two perfectly good jackets.
jokes.setup: Marge points out he ruined a jacket
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals he destroyed two jackets to make this abomination
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer made the situation twice as bad as assumed

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you ruined that jacket!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No I didn't, Marge. I destroyed two jackets to make this abomination.

jokes.text: Yes, Lisa. Daddy's a teacher.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks sophisticated questions about teaching methodology
jokes.punchline: Homer completely ignores the complexity and gives a simple non-answer
jokes.explanation: Shows the intellectual gap between Homer and Lisa efficiently

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, what's your philosophy on teaching methodology and student engagement?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! Teaching? I dunno, just tell 'em stuff until they stop asking questions.

jokes.text: Sir, it's a felony to tease the order box.
jokes.setup: Homer approaches drive-thru just to boast
jokes.punchline: Worker threatens legal action for not ordering
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of minor annoyance to criminal offense

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'll just have a large coffee... just kidding! I'm not ordering anything. I just wanted to drive up here and tell you that I'm the greatest driver in Springfield!

dialogue.speaker: Drive-thru worker
dialogue.line: Sir, you need to order something or leave.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But I'm not done bragging yet!

dialogue.speaker: Drive-thru worker
dialogue.line: That's it. If you don't order or leave right now, I'm calling the police and we'll sue you for harassment.

jokes.text: It's all right. I'm a teacher!
jokes.setup: Homer commits traffic violations and crimes
jokes.punchline: He thinks being a teacher gives him special privileges
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's inflated sense of importance from his new job

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm a teacher now? That means I can do whatever I want!

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: Sir, that's not how this works.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I saw it on a bumper sticker once. 'Teachers are heroes.' Heroes don't follow traffic laws!

jokes.text: I didn't know we could do that.
jokes.setup: Homer claims teacher privileges
jokes.punchline: Another driver is convinced and follows suit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's influence spreading chaos

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm a teacher, so I get to do whatever I want on the road.

dialogue.speaker: Other driver
dialogue.line: Oh, a teacher! In that case, I'm a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Other driver
dialogue.line: Excuse me!

jokes.text: Down the hall, Room 12. - [Mouth Full] Thank you. Ew. Okay. Let's get started.
jokes.setup: Homer directing lost student
jokes.punchline: Student has mouth full of food, speaks disgustingly
jokes.explanation: Low-level gross-out humor, not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Can I help you find something?

dialogue.speaker: Lost Student
dialogue.line: Yeah, *muffled sounds with mouth full* I'm looking for the cafeteria.

jokes.text: Yeah. Yeah! That'll eat up some time.
jokes.setup: Student suggests sharing relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Homer openly admits he has no lesson plan and needs to fill time
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's lack of preparation and honesty about it

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Why don't you tell us about your relationship problems?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I don't have a lesson plan, and I need to fill the time.

jokes.text: Whoa. You think you got 'em all, but you forget about the eggs.
jokes.setup: Otto is explaining his high standards in relationships
jokes.punchline: He gets distracted by killing a bug and talks about insect eggs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character moment - distracted and gross but delivered with authority

dialogue.speaker: Otto
dialogue.line: Yeah man, I got really high standards when it comes to chicks. Like, they gotta be into music, ya know? And they gotta like, respect the bus and stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Otto
dialogue.line: Whoa, dude! There's a bug on the wall, man. Hold on a second.

dialogue.speaker: Otto
dialogue.line: Got him! Haha! Excellent! You know, these bugs, they lay like thousands of eggs, man. Thousands! Can you imagine that? One bug, thousands of babies. That's insane, dude.

jokes.text: [Crushes Insect] Whoa. You think you got 'em all, but you forget about the eggs.
jokes.setup: Otto talking about high standards
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's exterminating bugs during class
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character moment - disgusting but delivered with philosophical tone

dialogue.speaker: Otto
dialogue.line: I got high standards, man. I don't just let any bugs hang around in my bus.

dialogue.speaker: Otto
dialogue.line: So I spray for 'em during class.

jokes.text: [Whispering] [Giggling] Okay. Here's a set of house keys and my A.T.M. card.
jokes.setup: Woman admits she makes bad choices
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves it by giving stranger her keys and ATM card
jokes.explanation: Good immediate demonstration of stated character flaw

dialogue.speaker: Female Student
dialogue.line: I make bad choices.

dialogue.speaker: Female Student
dialogue.line: Here, take my keys and ATM card.

jokes.text: Smithers' romantic fantasy about Mr. Burns
jokes.setup: Smithers shares his marriage problems
jokes.punchline: He reveals his romantic obsession with his boss through fantasy dialogue
jokes.explanation: Great character development moment that reveals Smithers' psychology

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: My wife just doesn't understand me. She says I'm always talking about Mr. Burns.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: But she doesn't realize that Mr. Burns is the only one who truly appreciates my dedication.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Sir, I've rearranged your schedule so we can spend more time together.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Excellent work, Smithers. You know me better than anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: That's because I love you, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: And I love you too, Smithers.

jokes.text: For you see, marriage is a lot like an orange.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to justify eating an orange during class
jokes.punchline: He creates an absurd metaphor comparing marriage to fruit
jokes.explanation: Good improvisation showing Homer's ability to BS his way through situations

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: It's not an orange, it's a metaphor for marriage. See, the skin is tough on the outside, but sweet on the inside. And if you squeeze it too hard, it falls apart.

jokes.text: If I wanted to see a man eat an orange, I would have taken the orange-eating class.
jokes.setup: Homer's orange metaphor fails to convince
jokes.punchline: Student points out the absurdity by referencing an equally absurd alternative class
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response that highlights the absurdity of adult education offerings

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: But Mr. Simpson, by that logic, couldn't I just take a class on how to be an orange?

jokes.text: Oh, I told Marge this wouldn't work the other night in bed.
jokes.setup: Students are leaving Homer's failed class
jokes.punchline: Homer inadvertently mentions his bedroom conversations with Marge
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot device - Homer's tendency to overshare intimate details

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, class, I guess you all learned that education is important.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Just like Marge always says to me in the bedroom...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, what was I talking about?

jokes.text: So somethin' wasn't workin' in bed, huh? Heh, heh, heh. - No, that's not what I meant.
jokes.setup: Student assumes sexual problems
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent confusion
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding setup for what follows

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, I think you have a problem... with your wife.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: My wife? What kind of problem?

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Well, you know... in the bedroom.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The bedroom? There's nothing wrong with our bedroom. We just got a new Comforpedic mattress!

jokes.text: She's been as gray as a mule since she was 17.
jokes.setup: Homer reveals Marge dyes her hair
jokes.punchline: He gives an unflattering and specific comparison about her natural color
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's lack of filter about personal details

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I know you dye your hair.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What?! How did you find out?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Your natural color came in at the roots. It's the same color as a dead pigeon.

jokes.text: Wait! I need closure on that anecdote.
jokes.setup: Homer gets arrested mid-story
jokes.punchline: Even the fast-food worker is invested in Homer's personal stories
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how compelling Homer's oversharing has become

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: So there I was, right, and Lenny says to me—

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: You're under arrest!

dialogue.speaker: Drive-through speaker
dialogue.line: Wait, wait! What happened next?!

jokes.text: Nah, nah, nah. You got it wrong, 'Pu. She's blue 56.
jokes.setup: Apu mentions Marge's hair dye color
jokes.punchline: Another customer corrects him with the specific shade number
jokes.explanation: Shows how widespread knowledge of Marge's personal details has become

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Mrs. Simpson, your hair color is looking particularly... auburn today.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Actually, it's Clairol Number 47: Marge Simpson Blue.

jokes.text: And I'm not out of order. You're out of order! The whole freakin' system is out of order. You want the truth? You want the truth? You can't handle the truth!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his teaching methods
jokes.punchline: String of movie quotes completely out of context
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference cascade, Homer using movie quotes as defense mechanism

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: My teaching methods may be unconventional, but they work. Now if you'll excuse me, I have some wisdom to impart: 'Here's looking at you, kid.' 'May the Force be with you.' 'You can't handle the truth!' 'I'll be back.' See? Pure pedagogy.

jokes.text: Forget it, Marge. It's Chinatown!
jokes.setup: Homer continues dramatic movie quotes
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate Chinatown reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd movie reference that makes no sense in context

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: She's my daughter AND my sister!

jokes.text: Webster's Dictionary describes a wedding as 'the process of removing weeds from one's garden.'
jokes.setup: Homer tries to start class properly with a dictionary definition
jokes.punchline: He confuses 'wedding' with 'weeding'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that shows Homer's lack of preparation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Alright class, let's start with a dictionary definition. Wedding: the removal of unwanted plants from a garden.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, that's weeding.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that explains why Marge was so mad on our honeymoon.

jokes.text: I can't believe I paid $10,000 for this course.
jokes.setup: Students are disappointed with Homer's teaching
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurdly high cost of this worthless adult education course
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the financial stakes and educational system satire

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, this course is a complete waste of time. You don't know anything about the subject matter.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I know one thing - you just paid $500 for this class.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's right. Five hundred dollars. Non-refundable.

jokes.text: I can't believe I paid $10,000 for this course. - What the heck was that lab fee for?
jokes.setup: Students disappointed with Homer's teaching
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high cost for marriage advice class
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing adult education pricing absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Student 1
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, we paid $500 for this marriage advice class?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's right. And it's worth every penny.

dialogue.speaker: Student 2
dialogue.line: But you've been married three times!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly. I'm an expert on what not to do.

dialogue.speaker: Student 1
dialogue.line: We want our money back!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Too late. Non-refundable. Now, who wants to hear about the time I forgot our anniversary and Marge didn't speak to me for a week?

jokes.text: So, anyway, Mr. 'X' would say, 'Marge, if this doesn't get your motor running... my name isn't Homer J. Simpson.'
jokes.setup: Homer tries to anonymize his stories with fake names
jokes.punchline: He immediately reveals their real identities in the quote
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure showing Homer's complete inability to maintain anonymity

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So there was this guy, let's call him... 'Jeb.' Jeb worked at this place, let's call it... 'The Nuclear Power Plant.' And Jeb was married to a woman named 'Marge' — I mean, 'Barb.'

jokes.text: They're going to observe the human peep show that is our lives.
jokes.setup: Homer brings his entire class home for dinner
jokes.punchline: He frames their family life as entertainment for voyeurs
jokes.explanation: Great phrase that captures the uncomfortable voyeuristic element Homer has created

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Welcome to my home, class! I've set up cameras in every room so you can watch us live our lives. It's like a reality show, except nobody cares about us!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, that's a huge invasion of privacy.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Invasion? Lisa, it's called 'The Simpsons: Live!' I'm getting paid in Duff beer and pork chops.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, take those cameras down right now!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Marge, we're entertainment! People love watching families suffer. It's the American dream!

jokes.text: Me and Milhouse took some mail from a mail truck and threw it down the sewer.
jokes.setup: Homer asks Bart about his day in front of the class
jokes.punchline: Bart casually admits to federal crimes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's casual attitude toward serious misbehavior

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So Bart, how was your day at school?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh, you know, Dad. The usual. Failed a test, got detention, committed a few federal crimes.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's nice, son.

jokes.text: You're the one who double-dared us.
jokes.setup: Homer scolds Bart for the mail incident
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals Homer instigated the federal crime
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Homer's hypocrisy and poor parenting

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Bart, what were you thinking?! You can't just mess with the mail!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Dad, you told me to do it!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I did no such thing!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yes you did. You said, 'Bart, go mess with the mail.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh. Well... that's different.

jokes.text: Hey, Homer. Why don't you just nibble her elbow? That always melts her butter, right? Heh, heh, heh.
jokes.setup: Student references Homer's earlier intimate revelation
jokes.punchline: Uses the embarrassing detail to taunt Homer
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing consequences of Homer's oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, remember when you said you cried during The Lion King?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! I told you that in confidence!

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Yeah, and now the whole school knows you're a big softie!

jokes.text: Hey, Homer. Why don't you just nibble her elbow? That always melts her butter, right? Heh, heh, heh.
jokes.setup: Student using Homer's intimate revelations
jokes.punchline: Suggests Homer perform intimate act publicly
jokes.explanation: Payoff for Homer's oversharing, creates maximum embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Hey Homer, remember when you told me about that thing you do with Marge?

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: What? No! I never told you that!

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, you should do it at the town meeting tomorrow. In front of everyone.

jokes.text: For tomorrow, you should read pages seven through 18 in Lisa's diary.
jokes.setup: Homer dismisses class after the dinner disaster
jokes.punchline: He assigns homework involving violating his daughter's privacy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Homer has learned nothing and will continue exploiting his family

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Alright, class, that's enough for today. For homework, I want you all to go home and read your sister's diary.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, that's not appropriate!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Shh, Lisa, this is educational.

jokes.text: Is any of this gonna be on the test? 'Cause I wasn't payin' attention.
jokes.setup: After dramatic family meltdown
jokes.punchline: Student only concerned about grades
jokes.explanation: Good timing, deflates dramatic tension with mundane student concern

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: But what about my grades?

jokes.text: Ah, Marge is throwin' me out for blabbin' about her elbow thing.
jokes.setup: Ned asks why Homer is outside
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately shares more intimate details with a neighbor
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Homer hasn't learned his lesson - he immediately overshares again

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Homer, what are you doing out here?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, hey Ned. Well, if you must know, I've been having some trouble in the bedroom. Marge and I haven't been intimate in weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Oh my...

jokes.text: Lisa's ball of string in the attic
jokes.setup: Lisa explains her coping mechanism for family stress
jokes.punchline: She has a massive ball of string showing this is a frequent occurrence
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that reveals Lisa's stress management and family dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: When the stress of family life gets to be too much, I have a coping mechanism.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I wind it all up into a ball of string.

jokes.text: Don't mind me, boys. Just scrubbin' my undies. - Sorry, Bart. Your dad kinda blew the fantasy.
jokes.setup: Boys playing sci-fi fantasy
jokes.punchline: Homer ruins it by discussing underwear washing
jokes.explanation: Homer's domestic reality interrupting children's imagination

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: We're in deep space, millions of miles from Earth. Our ship's engines are failing.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We'll have to eject the cargo to lighten the load.

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Good thinking! We might make it!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: By the way, Marge washed my underwear with her delicates again. Now they're all pink and stretched out.

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: ...Homer, we're in space.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know, that's why I'm telling you. Your mom's not here.

jokes.text: Without a strong male presence in the house, you could turn sissy overnight.
jokes.setup: Homer encourages Bart to keep roughhousing
jokes.punchline: He says this while doing laundry in women's clothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic juxtaposition between Homer's words and his emasculated situation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's it, boy! Keep roughhousing with your old man!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Builds character!

jokes.text: Technically, we're not allowed to go to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy dismisses most things as sins when asked about divorce
jokes.punchline: He reveals even basic bodily functions are prohibited by strict interpretation
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist take on religious extremism delivered with perfect deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Divorce? Well, that's a sin, of course. So is remarriage, covetousness, and a lot of other things...

dialogue.speaker: Parishioner
dialogue.line: What about breathing?

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Well, yes, technically that's a sin too if you interpret the scriptures strictly enough.

jokes.text: [Homer's Voice] I love you. Will you marry me, Marge? You mean, I'm gonna be a daddy? I hope we'll always be together, together, together--
jokes.setup: Marge remembering romantic moments with Homer
jokes.punchline: Memory gets stuck like broken record
jokes.explanation: Memory malfunction gag showing how nostalgia can become repetitive

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Remember when you brought me flowers and said you'd love me forever?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'd love you forever, I'd love you forever, I'd love you forever...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, why do you keep repeating that?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'd love you forever, I'd love you forever, I'd love you forever...

jokes.text: I've created a replacement that's superior to her in almost every way.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he doesn't need Marge anymore
jokes.punchline: His replacement is clearly just a houseplant
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Homer's complete break from reality

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't need Marge anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What? Homer, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've got everything I need right here.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This plant waters itself, it doesn't complain, and it makes the house look nice. Perfect wife!

jokes.text: Dad, that's just a plant. - Lisa! You will respect your new mother. Now, give her a kiss.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out obvious reality
jokes.punchline: Homer doubles down, demands Lisa treat plant as stepmother
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of delusion, Homer's authoritative tone makes absurdity funnier

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, that's not even a real plant.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Lisa, I don't want to hear it. That plant is your stepmother now, and I demand you treat her with respect.

jokes.text: Ah, your flesh mother used to bring me pudding.
jokes.setup: Lisa bringing Homer pudding
jokes.punchline: Homer refers to Marge as 'flesh mother' while talking to plant
jokes.explanation: Creepy and funny term showing Homer's deteriorating mental state

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, I brought you some pudding.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Thanks, sweetie. You know, this plant and I were just talking about how your mother makes the best pudding. Isn't that right, my green friend? Your flesh mother really knows her way around a dessert.

jokes.text: Eat the pudding. Eat the pudding-- [Continues rapidly]
jokes.setup: Homer tries to think of what makes him special to Marge
jokes.punchline: His brain immediately gets distracted by pudding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment showing his inability to focus on important thoughts

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What makes me special to Marge? Well, I'm... I'm... Mmm, pudding.

jokes.text: I heard you and Homer broke up, so I'm declarin' my intentions to move in on his territory. Here, I, uh, brung ya some posies.
jokes.setup: Moe making romantic overture to Marge
jokes.punchline: Crude territorial language and 'brung ya some posies'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - crude but trying to be romantic

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Back off, Simpson! She's mine now! I brung ya some posies.

jokes.text: I come here, I get dressed up all nice-like... put my heart on the line, and I make a fool of myself.
jokes.setup: Moe gets rejected by Marge
jokes.punchline: He delivers an unexpectedly vulnerable and articulate response
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Moe's surprising emotional depth

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Look, Marge, I know I'm not much to look at. I got a face like a catcher's mitt and a personality to match. But I got a heart, you know? And it's yours if you want it. I just... I just wanted you to know that you mean something to me.

jokes.text: I didn't ask her for no water. She's lyin', Homer. She told me you were dead. That's the only reason I-- I didn't do nothin'!
jokes.setup: Homer finds Moe at his house
jokes.punchline: Moe's panicked over-explanation and lies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Moe's panic and improvised excuses

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Moe? What are you doing at my house?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer! I can explain! I was just... uh... checking your... gutters! Yeah, your gutters needed checking and I'm a gutter checker now. It's a new business. Very legitimate. I have a license!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: And before you ask, no, I don't know anything about your stuff being missing. That wasn't me. I was here the whole time checking gutters. Gutters, Homer! That's what I do!

jokes.text: She told me you were dead. That's the only reason I-- I didn't do nothin'!
jokes.setup: Homer catches Moe at his house
jokes.punchline: Moe's increasingly frantic and unconvincing denials
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Moe's desperation and poor lying skills

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Moe! What are you doing at my house?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer! Hey there, pal. I was just, uh... passing by.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Passing by? You're inside my living room.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Right, right. I came in to use the phone. My bar phone's broken.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: The phone's right there, Moe.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Oh, that one? No, no, I needed YOUR phone specifically. It's a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Moe, what's really going on?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Nothing! Why would anything be going on? I'm just a regular guy doing regular guy things in your house at night with the lights off and your wife's jewelry box open!

jokes.text: Oh. My tattered rags... are caught on your coffee table.
jokes.setup: Homer making dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Gets stuck on furniture in pathetic way
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy undermining Homer's dramatic moment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's it! I'm leaving this house forever!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And I'm not coming back until things change around here!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Goodbye!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oof! Ow! My butt's stuck on the couch! D'oh!

jokes.text: I know now what I can offer you that no one else can: complete and utter dependence.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes what makes him unique to Marge
jokes.punchline: His special quality is total helplessness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant resolution that's both funny and touching - captures the core of their relationship

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I realized what makes me special.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What's that, Homie?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Total helplessness. I can't do anything without you.

jokes.text: complete and utter dependence. - Homer, that's not a good thing. - Are you kidding? It's a wondrous, marvelous thing.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes what he offers Marge
jokes.punchline: Thinks complete dependence is a positive trait
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's complete lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I just realized what I offer you.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What's that, Homie?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Complete dependence. You need me for everything — money, decisions, even remembering where you put things. That's love, Marge. That's what keeps us together.

jokes.text: We've been separated for a day, and I'm as dirty as a Frenchman.
jokes.setup: Homer explains why Marge can trust him
jokes.punchline: He uses an ethnic stereotype to describe his hygiene decline
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup but relies on dated stereotype for the punchline

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You can trust me, Marge. I'm like a locksmith—I know all the combinations.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And my hygiene? Well, let's just say I'm going full 'unwashed Italian restaurant owner.'

jokes.text: Wait till my class hears about this. Kidding!
jokes.setup: Homer has an intimate moment with Marge
jokes.punchline: He jokes about oversharing again, showing he's learned his lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows character growth while maintaining the comedy pattern

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! That was amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, not so loud. The kids might hear.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, don't worry. I learned my lesson about oversharing. I'm not gonna tell Lenny and Carl about how you—

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: See? I stopped myself. I'm a changed man.

jokes.text: Hey, this is dated two weeks ago. - Oh. Sorry. Here's a fresh one.
jokes.setup: Bart shows Homer a failing grade to celebrate his return
jokes.punchline: The grade is old, and Bart has an even fresher failure ready
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bart's consistent academic failure and Homer's return to normalcy

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Welcome home, Dad! I got an F!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: An F? Why you little—

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, wait! That's from last week. Check out today's!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why, that's even worse!

tmdb_overview: Homer teaches a class on marriage, but sharing secrets about his own union gets him kicked out of the house.
insight: Homer's marital crisis spawns 71 jokes in 22 minutes—high volume masks middling impact ratings.
